content: We can hypothesize that integrating multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives (from LLM self-reflection mechanisms research) into the original Self-Refine framework will unlock higher-quality output refinement without requiring additional training or external supervision. This creates a 'multi-lens self-refinement' framework that addresses a key unspoken limitation of vanilla Self-Refine: over-reliance on a single, biased self-feedback perspective that can get stuck in local optimization loops.
